title: India Weather Update Today June 10: Check Latest Rain and Heat Wave Alert, Current Temperatures of Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Bengaluru, Hyderabad - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
India faces a sharp weather divide on June 10, 2026, as severe heatwave conditions persist across Northwest India with temperatures touching 45–46°C, while the advancing Southwest Monsoon brings heavy rain, thunderstorms, flood risks, and strong winds to northeastern, southern, and eastern regions.
Severe heatwave conditions continue to scorch parts of Northwest India today, June 10, 2026, while the Southwest Monsoon rapidly advances across the southern, western, and northeastern regions, bringing heavy rainfall. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued specific alerts tracking a stark weather contrast across the country.
Delhi: 36°C
Mumbai: 33°C
Hyderabad: 31°C
Bengaluru: 24°C
Chennai: 34°C
Ahmedabad: 35°C
Kolkata: 33°C
Patna: 34°C
Jaipur: 36°C
Temperatures recorded on June 10, 10:00 AM
Northeast India and the Sub-Himalayan region of West Bengal are expected to witness heavy to very heavy rainfall, particularly across Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, and Sikkim. These conditions may trigger landslides in vulnerable hilly areas, reduce visibility, and increase the risk of flash flooding.
In the south, Kerala, Karnataka, and parts of Telangana remain under heavy rain alerts, while rough sea conditions along the Kerala-Karnataka coast have prompted warnings for fishermen. Meanwhile, thunderstorms with strong winds are likely over Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha, and parts of Bihar. Across Delhi-NCR, Haryana, and Punjab, intense daytime heat may give way to evening thunderstorms, rain, lightning, and gusty winds reaching 60 kmph.
Active heatwave alerts remain in effect today, June 10, 2026, for isolated pockets across Northwest and Central India, with maximum temperatures ranging from 42°C to 45°C. According to the official India Meteorological Department (IMD) Bulletin, hot, dry northwesterly winds from the desert continue to drive up temperatures, though a major cool-down from an approaching Western Disturbance is expected to break the heatwave cycle starting tomorrow, June 11.
Heatwave conditions continue to grip Delhi-NCR, Haryana, and Punjab, with temperatures in and around Safdarjung expected to reach nearly 44°C. Dry, hot winds are intensifying discomfort across the region, although increasing cloud cover and the possibility of evening thunderstorms may provide some relief.
In West Rajasthan, areas including Sriganganagar and Barmer remain among the hottest locations, recording daytime temperatures between 45°C and 46°C. Meanwhile, parts of Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, and Himachal Pradesh are experiencing unusually high early-June temperatures, with some valleys recording temperatures 3°C to 5°C above normal. Along the coasts of Odisha, Konkan, and Goa, high humidity combined with temperatures above 36°C is creating oppressive and uncomfortable weather conditions.
